data.to: Hepatology Department, Milton Keynes University Hospital
data.from: Dr. A. Shah, Eaglestone Health Centre
data.reason_for_referral: Abnormal LFTs identified during routine diabetic annual review. Patient is asymptomatic. Multiple metabolic risk factors present.
relevant_past_medical_history: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (diagnosed 2020, poorly controlled HbA1c 7.8%)
data.alcohol_intake: Approximately 18 units per week. Beer and wine at weekends, occasional whisky.

data.chief_complaint: Routine diabetic annual review. No complaints. Feels well.
data.hpi: 51M with metabolic syndrome. Asymptomatic. LFTs found elevated on routine screening. Reports moderate alcohol use. Denies abdominal pain, jaundice, or weight change.

physical_examination.abdomen: Soft, obese, non-tender. Liver edge not palpable through adipose tissue.
physical_examination.skin: No jaundice. No stigmata of chronic liver disease.

data.assessment: Elevated LFTs in context of metabolic syndrome and moderate alcohol. Mixed hepatocellular/cholestatic pattern. Needs hepatology evaluation to differentiate MASLD vs alcohol-related vs other causes.

sections.title: Pre-Consultation Questionnaire
qa: None. Came for my diabetic check. Feel fine.
About 18 units per week. Mostly beer at weekends, sometimes a whisky in the evening.
Yes. Type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, overweight.
